records.ety: [Gr. <?/; <?/ = <?/ twice + ga`mma the letter . So called because it resembled two gammas placed one above the other.]

defs: A letter (<?/, <?/) of the Greek alphabet, which early fell into disuse.
notes: ☞ This form identifies it with the Latin F, though in sound it is said to have been nearer V. It was pronounced, probably, much like the English W.
